video.title: Fable Is Back: Claude Code, AI Agents & Fitness Tech Livestream
video.description:
An unedited livestream on where AI agents feel right now: Fable coming back, why Claude Code still feels like the strongest harness for my workflow, how Cursor comparisons can get messy, and why the model itself is only part of the story.
The big thread here is that LLMs do not really “edit videos” or “pull fitness data” by themselves. The harness, tools, APIs, local scripts, devices, and workflows around the model are where a huge amount of the intelligence actually shows up.
I also get into open/local models, Hermes agents, Google Antigravity, Project Aria, Agent Stack Daily, training dashboards, heart meds, running, lifting, and why jiu-jitsu is still worth doing even when it wrecks you.
Not medical, legal, financial, or training advice. Just personal experience, AI commentary, and live thinking out loud.

Chapters:
00:00 Fable Is Back and My Audio Is Better
02:29 The Fable Rollback and Degradation Claims
04:35 Claude Code vs Cursor for Fable
06:07 Why Fable Feels Ahead of Open Models
09:32 Model Access and Picking Winners
12:50 Minimax, Hermes, Cursor, and Bad Comparisons
15:36 Local Hardware: M3 Ultra, DGX Spark, and Exo
16:31 LLMs vs Tools: What Actually Edits Video
21:05 The LLM Is the Heart, the Harness Is the Brain
27:45 APIs, Devices, and Getting Fitness Data Out
34:22 Google Antigravity and Subscription Value
36:38 Training Volume, Aria, and Health Rebound
43:46 Hermes Agents, Project Aria, and Agent Stack Daily
52:12 Heart Meds, Running, and Feeling Like Myself Again
59:17 Why I Still Do Jiu-Jitsu  
#AIAgents #ClaudeCode #FitnessTech
